doc.title: Documentation
doc.getting_started_1: **Create a flow**: Go to the Editor and add tasks
doc.getting_started_2: **Configure**: Click each task to set its parameters
doc.getting_started_5: **Monitor**: Follow execution in real-time in Monitoring
doc.architecture_desc: **PawFlow** is an Apache NiFi-style framework for building complex data pipelines.
diff.no_saved_version: No saved version to compare against.
runtime.cron.format_hint: Format: minute hour day month weekday
doc.task_reference_desc: All available task types with their parameters and usage.
doc.expressions_desc: PawFlow supports dynamic expressions in task parameters.
doc.expressions_example: Example: `${filename}` resolves to the FlowFile's `filename` attribute
doc.services_desc: Services provide shared connections to external systems (databases, SFTP, HTTP, etc.).
doc.flowfile_desc: A FlowFile is the unit of data flowing through a pipeline. It has binary content and a dictionary of string attributes.
doc.api_desc: PawFlow exposes a REST API via FastAPI at `/docs` (Swagger UI) and `/redoc` (ReDoc).
doc.api_endpoints: 85+ endpoints covering flows, execution, monitoring, security, plugins, cluster, and more.
doc.plugins_desc: Package custom tasks, services, and flows as `.pfp` plugin archives for sharing and reuse.
editor.author: Author
runtime.cron.params_help: JSON dict of parameters to inject into the flow at each execution
doc.try_it_hint: Go to the Editor sidebar → Expression Tester to test expressions interactively.
